pattern: A bottom-up tree pass balances each node's children to their deepest path sum, charging one increment per shorter child because a single bump on an already-balanced subtree lifts all its leaf paths at once.
hints: Since cost can only go up, the longest existing root-to-leaf path through any node fixes the target every sibling subtree must reach.
If a child's subtree is already internally balanced, raising just that child by the right amount lifts all of its leaf paths uniformly, costing a single increased node.
Depth-first, return each node's balanced path sum (its cost plus the max child sum) and add one to a counter for every child whose returned sum is below that max.

stress.description: random rooted tree with varied costs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    edges = []
    for v in range(1, n):
        u = rng.randrange(0, v)
        edges.append([u, v])
    cost = [rng.randrange(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [n, edges, cost]
stress.note: parent < child keeps node 0 as root; iterative DFS handles depth
